message:
feat(suggestions): add fallback explanation system for patterns

Adds fallback explanation generator for patterns without explicit
explanations, ensuring no empty explanation sections in output.

Changes:
- Add `fallback_explanation(pack_id, pattern_name)` function
- Add `get_explanation(explicit, pack_id, pattern_name)` function
- Fallback mentions pack:pattern, points to `dcg explain`
- Neutral, concise language following style guide
- 8 unit tests covering all fallback scenarios
- Fix missing explanation field in confidence_scoring test

Closes git_safety_guard-r97e.2

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-joyb","title":"Command Telemetry: Configuration options","notes":"
---

## IMPORTANT: Default Configuration Change

**CHANGE REQUIRED**: Default `redaction_mode` should be `pattern` NOT `none` for security-by-default.

```toml
[telemetry]
enabled = false           # Opt-in
redaction_mode = "pattern"  # CHANGED from "none" - secure by default
retention_days = 90
max_size_mb = 500
```

Users who want raw commands must explicitly set `redaction_mode = "none"`.

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_default_config_values() {
    let config = TelemetryConfig::default();

    assert!(!config.enabled);                    // Off by default
    assert_eq!(config.redaction_mode, RedactionMode::Pattern);  // Secure default!
    assert_eq!(config.retention_days, 90);
    assert_eq!(config.max_size_mb, 500);
}

#[test]
fn test_config_from_toml() {
    let toml = r#"
        [telemetry]
        enabled = true
        redaction_mode = "full"
        retention_days = 30
    "#;

    let config: Config = toml::from_str(toml).unwrap();
    assert!(config.telemetry.enabled);
    assert_eq!(config.telemetry.redaction_mode, RedactionMode::Full);
    assert_eq!(config.telemetry.retention_days, 30);
}

#[test]
fn test_redaction_mode_parsing() {
    assert_eq!(RedactionMode::from_str("none").unwrap(), RedactionMode::None);
    assert_eq!(RedactionMode::from_str("pattern").unwrap(), RedactionMode::Pattern);
    assert_eq!(RedactionMode::from_str("full").unwrap(), RedactionMode::Full);
    assert!(RedactionMode::from_str("invalid").is_err());
}

#[test]
fn test_env_var_override() {
    std::env::set_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_ENABLED", "true");
    std::env::set_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_REDACTION_MODE", "full");

    let config = TelemetryConfig::from_env_and_file(None);
    assert!(config.enabled);
    assert_eq!(config.redaction_mode, RedactionMode::Full);

    std::env::remove_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_ENABLED");
    std::env::remove_var("DCG_TELEMETRY_REDACTION_MODE");
}

#[test]
fn test_database_path_expansion() {
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        database_path: Some("~/.config/dcg/telemetry.db".into()),
        ..Default::default()
    };

    let expanded = config.expanded_database_path();
    assert!(!expanded.to_string_lossy().contains("~"));
    assert!(expanded.to_string_lossy().contains("dcg"));
}

#[test]
fn test_retention_days_validation() {
    // Minimum retention
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        retention_days: 0,
        ..Default::default()
    };
    assert!(config.validate().is_err());

    // Maximum retention (prevent accidental huge values)
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        retention_days: 10000,
        ..Default::default()
    };
    assert!(config.validate().is_err() || config.retention_days == 10000);
}
```

### Integration Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn test_config_applied_to_telemetry_writer() {
    let config = TelemetryConfig {
        enabled: true,
        redaction_mode: RedactionMode::Full,
        ..Default::default()
    };

    let db = TelemetryDb::open_in_memory().unwrap();
    let writer = TelemetryWriter::new(&db, &config);

    writer.log(&CommandEntry {
        command: "curl -H 'Bearer secret123'".into(),
        ..Default::default()
    });
    writer.flush();

    let stored = db.query_recent(1).unwrap().pop().unwrap();
    assert_eq!(stored.command, "[REDACTED]");
}
```

### E2E Tests

```bash
# Test default redaction is 'pattern' (secure by default)
CONFIG=$(dcg config show --json | jq -r '.telemetry.redaction_mode')
[[ "$CONFIG" == "pattern" ]] && echo "PASS: Secure default" || echo "WARN: Check default"

# Test config file is respected
cat > /tmp/dcg_test_config.toml <<EOF
[telemetry]
enabled = true
retention_days = 7
EOF

DCG_CONFIG=/tmp/dcg_test_config.toml dcg telemetry status | grep -q "7 days" && \
    echo "PASS: Config respected"
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T00:41:28.917288585-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T03:38:24.861624903-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-14T03:38:24.861624903-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-joyb","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-9asp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-14T01:24:16.356907695-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","title":"Span Highlighting for Blocked Commands","description":"# Epic: Span Highlighting for Blocked Commands

## Priority Score: 820/1000 (Highest Priority)

## Background and Rationale

This feature addresses the number one user pain point with security tools: Why was my command blocked?

When DCG blocks a command, users currently see which pattern matched, but not exactly WHAT part of their command triggered the match. This leads to user frustration, perception of DCG as arbitrary, time wasted debugging, and reduced trust.

### Example of Proposed Output

When a destructive command is denied, show caret-style highlighting:

  command --dangerous-flag /sensitive/path
                           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
                           Path targeting system file

## Technical Approach

The match span data ALREADY EXISTS in DCG evaluation pipeline. PatternMatch captures match info and regex find() returns byte positions. We just need to surface this in the output.

## Implementation Steps

1. Extend EvaluationResult to carry match span information
2. Create terminal formatter for caret-style highlighting with ANSI colors  
3. Integrate into hook output (stderr warning on denial)
4. Add to dcg test output for consistency

## Key Files

- src/evaluator.rs - Ensure match spans captured
- src/hook.rs - Integrate highlighting into denial output
- src/cli.rs - Update dcg test output
- New: src/highlight.rs or extend src/trace.rs

## Success Criteria

- Denied commands show exactly which substring triggered the match
- Works correctly with multi-byte UTF-8 characters
- Works for compound commands (multiple matches shown)
- Colors disabled gracefully when not in TTY
- No performance regression

## Cross-Model Validation

Both Claude Opus 4.5 and comparison model rated this 820/1000. Strong consensus on value.","notes":"Keep dependency separation: Visual Explanations epic should not depend on span capture/highlighting tasks.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:03:51.357954793-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:16:42.362535647-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.1","title":"[Span] Ensure match spans captured for all match sources","description":"# Task: Ensure match spans captured for all match sources

## Background
`matched_span` exists in `EvaluationResult`, but not all match paths populate it consistently.
Span highlighting will be unreliable unless every match source provides a byte span
(or an explicit fallback) in terms of the original command string.

## Scope
Cover **all** match sources:
- Pack regex matches (safe/destructive patterns)
- Legacy patterns (if any remain in evaluator paths)
- Heredoc/AST matches (inline script detection)

## Approach
- Audit match creation in `src/evaluator.rs` and ensure each code path sets:
  - `matched_span` (byte offsets)
  - `matched_text_preview` (already present; keep for fallback)
- For pack regex matches, use `regex.find()` from the selected engine to capture start/end.
- For legacy patterns, capture span from the legacy regex if used.
- For AST/heredoc matches, map extracted script offsets back to the original command when possible
  (use extraction metadata / heredoc boundaries). If mapping is impossible, store a structured reason
  and keep `matched_text_preview` as the fallback.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/heredoc.rs` / `src/ast_matcher.rs` (mapping helpers, if needed)
- `src/trace.rs` (optional: include span mapping reason in trace)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every deny/warn match source sets a usable `matched_span`, or records a clear fallback reason.
- Span is based on **original command**, not just normalized/sanitized forms.
- No performance regression on the hot path.

## Notes
This task is a prerequisite for reliable span highlighting. Keep it independent of the
Visual Explanations epic.

-{"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.2","title":"[Span] UTF-8 safe slicing + windowing for long commands","description":"# Task: UTF-8 safe slicing + windowing for long commands

## Background
Highlighting needs to work on arbitrary UTF-8 input. Naive slicing can panic
on non-boundary indices, and long commands can create unreadable output.

## Scope
- UTF-8 boundary-safe slicing around match spans
- Ellipsis/windowing for long commands (show match context)

## Approach
- Create a helper that:
  - Accepts `command`, `matched_span`, and `max_width`
  - Returns a **windowed** command string and adjusted caret span
  - Preserves valid UTF-8 boundaries
- Ensure the caret line aligns with the windowed output.
- Prefer no allocations on short commands.

## Files
- `src/hook.rs` (formatter helpers)
- Optional: new `src/highlight.rs` to keep formatting utilities isolated

## Acceptance Criteria
- No panics on multi-byte characters
- Long commands are truncated with `...` while keeping the match visible
- Caret span aligns with windowed output (TTY and non-TTY)

## Notes
This is a core utility for the formatter. Keep it small and testable.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.3","title":"[Span] Respect TTY/non-TTY for highlighting output","description":"# Task: Respect TTY/non-TTY for highlighting output

## Background
DCG already disables ANSI colors when stderr is not a TTY. Highlight output
must follow the same rule so non-TTY logs remain clean and machine-readable.

## Scope
- Ensure caret/highlight formatting is correct in both modes
- Avoid ANSI sequences when `stderr` is not a TTY or when `NO_COLOR` is set

## Approach
- Use existing color control in `hook::configure_colors()` and `colored::control`.
- Provide a non-color fallback format (same caret positioning, no ANSI).
- Add tests or assertions in `deny_output_tests` if feasible.

## Files
- `src/hook.rs`
- `src/logging.rs` (if formatting helpers live there)

## Acceptance Criteria
- TTY: colored output with caret highlighting
- non-TTY: plain output with caret highlighting, no ANSI escape codes
- `NO_COLOR` respected
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.704625444-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T01:19:31.018966545-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T01:19:31.018966545-05:00","close_reason":"TTY/NO_COLOR infrastructure already implemented: configure_colors() checks is_terminal(), allow_once_should_colorize() checks NO_COLOR and TERM=dumb, colored::control::set_override(false) disables ANSI. Test test_formatting_respects_no_color_env verifies this. Formatter (hewe) will use this infrastructure.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:40.705902509-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.4","title":"[Span] Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands","description":"# Task: Preserve highlight accuracy for normalized/sanitized commands

## Background
DCG normalizes commands (wrapper stripping) and sanitizes safe spans.
If matching uses the normalized/sanitized string, highlight spans can drift
from the original command shown to users.

## Scope
- Ensure highlighted spans refer to the **original** command text
- Handle cases where normalization changes offsets

## Approach
- Track mapping between original and normalized/sanitized command when matches
  are computed on modified strings.
- If mapping is complex, compute match on the original command when safe
  (e.g., re-run regex on original for span only).
- Document fallback behavior when mapping is impossible.

## Files
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- `src/normalize.rs`
- `src/context.rs` / `src/trace.rs` (if mapping metadata stored)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Highlighted spans point to correct substring in original command
- No regression in decision logic (matching stays as before)
- Edge cases documented (e.g., when only preview is available)

Test match_span_maps_to_original_with_wrappers verifies correctness.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:15:47.426703447-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-jpfm.5","title":"[Span] Unit tests for span capture + mapping","description":"# Task: Unit tests for span capture + mapping

## Purpose
Validate that every match source (pack/legacy/heredoc) produces a correct span
in original-command coordinates.

## Requirements
- Tests for pack regex span capture
- Tests for normalization/sanitization offset mapping
- Tests for heredoc/AST span mapping or fallback behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests cover multiple match sources
- Failures include detailed diagnostics of expected vs actual spans

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qb4b","title":"[PACK] cloud.gcp: container registry destructive patterns","description":"# GCP container registry gaps\n\n## Scope\nAdd destructive patterns to cloud.gcp pack for container registry operations.\n\n## Destructive patterns (examples)\n- gcloud container images delete\n- gcloud artifacts docker images delete\n- gcloud artifacts repositories delete\n\n## Acceptance criteria\n- [ ] Patterns added with reasons + tests\n- [ ] Keywords updated if needed (artifacts, images)\n- [ ] E2E pack cases added","notes":"Updates in src/packs/cloud/gcp.rs: added keywords (artifacts/images/repositories) and destructive patterns for gcloud container images delete, gcloud artifacts docker images delete, and gcloud artifacts repositories delete; added unit tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:51.046413564-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:39:50.629784403-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:39:50.629784403-05:00","close_reason":"Patterns + tests added"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qdhh","title":"[DESIGN] GitHub/GitLab Pack Scope Delineation","description":"# GitHub/GitLab Pack Scope Delineation

## Problem
We have overlapping packs that both cover `gh` and `glab` commands:
- `platform.github` / `platform.gitlab` - Broad platform operations
- `cicd.github_actions` / `cicd.gitlab_ci` - CI/CD specific operations

## Proposed Resolution

### Merge Approach (RECOMMENDED)
Merge the platform and cicd packs for each provider:
- `github.*` single pack covering all `gh` commands
- `gitlab.*` single pack covering all `glab` commands

**Rationale**: 
- Users think in terms of 'GitHub commands' not 'platform vs CI'
- Simpler mental model
- No risk of pattern duplication or gaps
- Easier to maintain

### Implementation
1. Close `platform.github` (git_safety_guard-dxs) and `platform.gitlab` (git_safety_guard-h5j) categories
2. Close `platform.github` (git_safety_guard-3w5) and `platform.gitlab` (git_safety_guard-z2q) pack tasks
3. Rename `cicd.github_actions` to `github` and expand scope
4. Rename `cicd.gitlab_ci` to `gitlab` and expand scope
5. Move expanded packs to appropriate category (or create new `vcs.*` category)

## Decision Required
- [ ] Team agrees on merge approach
- [ ] Update affected beads
- [ ] Document in architecture decisions

## Dependencies
- Blocks: Implementation of any GitHub/GitLab packs","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:33:33.787725725-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T16:03:57.151825718-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T16:03:57.151825718-05:00","close_reason":"DECIDED: Keep packs separate but clarify boundaries. cicd.github_actions covers CI operations (secrets, variables, workflows, runs). platform.github covers platform operations (repos, releases, deploy keys, webhooks, collaborators). Updated platform.github pack description to explicitly list what's NOT in cicd pack."}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qff","title":"[PACK] featureflags.flipt - Flipt Pack","description":"# Flipt Feature Flags Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Flipt (open-source feature flags) operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### flipt CLI
- `flipt flag delete`
- `flipt segment delete`
- `flipt namespace delete`

### API Operations
- `curl -X DELETE` to Flipt API
- gRPC delete operations

### Database Operations
- Direct SQL delete on Flipt database

## Safe Patterns
- `flipt flag list`
- `flipt flag get`
- `flipt evaluate`
- GET API requests

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: featureflags.* (git_safety_guard-ekc)
- Keywords: flipt
- Note: Open-source, often self-hosted

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for flag deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for namespace deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/featureflags_flipt.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag deletion blocked
- [ ] Namespace deletion blocked
- [ ] Evaluation allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:01.580796394-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T04:53:55.506340935-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T04:53:55.506340935-05:00","close_reason":"Pack already implemented and tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.642907646-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.688656814-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.733908616-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.779547847-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.826356288-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:41.87102903-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.496859749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qff","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:12.54374348-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-qqsl","title":"[Explain] Write explanations for core.git patterns","description":"# Task: Write explanations for core.git patterns

## Parent Epic: Visual Explanations (git_safety_guard-r97e)

## Objective

Write detailed explanations for all destructive patterns in the core.git pack. Git commands are frequently blocked by DCG, so these explanations are high-visibility.

## Patterns to Document

Review src/packs/core/git.rs for patterns including:
- git reset --hard
- git checkout -- (file)
- git clean -f
- git push --force  
- git branch -D
- git stash drop/clear
- git rebase (history rewrite)

## Example Explanations

### reset-hard
Explanation: |
  git reset --hard discards all uncommitted changes in your working
  directory and staging area. This cannot be undone - changes that
  were never committed are permanently lost.

  Safer alternatives:
  - git stash: Saves changes for later retrieval
  - git reset --soft: Keeps changes in working directory
  - git reset --mixed: Keeps changes unstaged (default)

  Preview what would be lost:
    git diff && git status

### push-force  
Explanation: |
  Force pushing overwrites remote history, potentially destroying
  commits that others have already pulled. This can cause data loss
  for your entire team and requires manual recovery.

  Safer alternative:
  - git push --force-with-lease: Only forces if no one else pushed

  Check remote state first:
    git fetch && git log origin/branch..HEAD

## File Location

src/packs/core/git.rs

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every git destructive pattern has explanation
- Explanations mention data loss consequences
- All patterns have safer alternatives
- Preview commands for checking state

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-qqsl","title":"[Explain] Write explanations for core.git patterns","description":"# Task: Write explanations for core.git patterns

## Parent Epic: Visual Explanations (git_safety_guard-r97e)

## Objective

Write detailed explanations for all destructive patterns in the core.git pack. Git commands are frequently blocked by DCG, so these explanations are high-visibility.

## Patterns to Document

Review src/packs/core/git.rs for patterns including:
- git reset --hard
- git checkout -- (file)
- git clean -f
- git push --force  
- git branch -D
- git stash drop/clear
- git rebase (history rewrite)

## Example Explanations

### reset-hard
Explanation: |
  git reset --hard discards all uncommitted changes in your working
  directory and staging area. This cannot be undone - changes that
  were never committed are permanently lost.

  Safer alternatives:
  - git stash: Saves changes for later retrieval
  - git reset --soft: Keeps changes in working directory
  - git reset --mixed: Keeps changes unstaged (default)

  Preview what would be lost:
    git diff && git status

### push-force  
Explanation: |
  Force pushing overwrites remote history, potentially destroying
  commits that others have already pulled. This can cause data loss
  for your entire team and requires manual recovery.

  Safer alternative:
  - git push --force-with-lease: Only forces if no one else pushed

  Check remote state first:
    git fetch && git log origin/branch..HEAD

## File Location

src/packs/core/git.rs

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every git destructive pattern has explanation
- Explanations mention data loss consequences
- All patterns have safer alternatives
- Preview commands for checking state

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","title":"[PACK] cicd.gitlab_ci - GitLab CI Pack","description":"# GitLab CI Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for GitLab CI/CD variables and pipeline operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `glab variable delete` - Removes CI variables
- `glab ci delete` - Deletes pipeline artifacts
- `glab api -X DELETE .../variables/`
- `gitlab-runner unregister` - Unregisters runners

## Safe Patterns
- `glab variable list`
- `glab ci list`, `glab ci view`
- `glab ci status`
- `gitlab-runner list`, `gitlab-runner status`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: cicd.* (git_safety_guard-0v8)
- Keywords: glab, gitlab-runner
- Overlaps with platform.gitlab for broader GitLab operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Variable deletion blocked
- [ ] Runner unregistration blocked
- [ ] Pipeline operations handled
- [ ] Tests cover common GitLab CI workflows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:27.39470727-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:42:06.751253341-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:42:06.751253341-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented GitLab CI pack, registered in cicd module + registry, added tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.056604561-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.096505335-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.138085769-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.178316444-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.222956114-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.261737141-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.765534832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qr6","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.811421627-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","title":"[TASK] Performance regression testing for packs","description":"# Performance Regression Testing for Packs

## Scope
Ensure adding new packs doesn't degrade dcg performance.

## Deliverables

### 1. Performance Benchmark Suite
Create `benches/pack_performance.rs`:
```rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, Criterion};

fn bench_single_pack_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark each pack individually
}

fn bench_all_packs_evaluation(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark with all packs enabled
}

fn bench_pattern_matching(c: &mut Criterion) {
    // Benchmark regex pattern matching specifically
}
```

### 2. Performance Budgets
Define maximum acceptable times:
- Single pack evaluation: < 500µs
- All packs evaluation: < 5ms
- Regex pattern match: < 100µs per pattern
- Total hook execution: < 10ms

### 3. CI Performance Tracking
- Run benchmarks on every PR
- Compare against baseline
- Fail PR if regression > 10%
- Track performance over time (grafana/dashboard)

### 4. Performance Logging
When DEBUG enabled:
- Log time per pack
- Log time per pattern
- Identify slow patterns
- Suggest optimizations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark suite covers all performance-critical paths
- [ ] Budgets defined and enforced in CI
- [ ] Performance tracked over time
- [ ] Slow patterns flagged for optimization","notes":"Context (perf reality check):
- Current process-per-command baseline (release) is ~173–179ms p50 for normal hook invocations because pack registry initialization compiles regexes.
- Criterion benches (in-process) are still useful, but they do NOT capture process startup + per-invocation regex compilation cost.

Plan adjustments:
- Treat `git_safety_guard-64dc` (lazy registry + lazy compilation) as the prerequisite that makes pack performance budgets meaningful.
- After startup costs are eliminated, criterion benches can focus on steady-state evaluation cost (sanitize/normalize/pack matching).
- Add a process-level harness (JSON baseline + diff) via `git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3` so CI can gate on p95/p99 for the allow path.

Budget note:
- The "Total hook execution < 10ms" budget is a good target but should be enforced only after the startup refactor lands, otherwise it will fail constantly and become noise.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:34:15.054793749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:39:21.26259381-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:39:21.26259381-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: Updated heredoc_perf.rs to include cicd and safe.cleanup packs in worst-case scenarios, verified performance remains within budgets.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-64dc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:54:17.803032297-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ksk.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:54:22.89206455-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-qyav","title":"[BUG][P1] pack_aware_quick_reject misses rm when args are /tmp or quoted ","description":"Regression: golden rm parity test shows allow for commands that should be denied (e.g. rm -r -f "/foo"). Suspect pack_aware_quick_reject_with_normalized fails to detect 'rm' keyword in executable spans when targets are /tmp or quoted , causing Step 4 quick-reject in evaluator to allow early. Fix quick-reject keyword gating to be conservative for rm/gits command words and add unit tests covering /tmp + quoted TMPDIR variants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-11T05:18:30.416258064-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T06:04:36.386761667-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T06:04:36.386761667-05:00","close_reason":"Fix quick-reject + normalization for rm /tmp and quoted TMPDIR variants; added regression tests; full gates + e2e green."}

+name: Notify ACFS checksum monitor
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main, master]
+    paths:
+      - 'install.sh'
+      - 'scripts/install.sh'
+  release:
+    types: [published]
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+jobs:
+  dispatch:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Dispatch to ACFS
+        uses: peter-evans/repository-dispatch@v3
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN }}
+          repository: Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
+          event-type: upstream-changed
+          client-payload: '{"repo":"${{ github.repository }}","ref":"${{ github.ref }}","sha":"${{ github.sha }}","event":"${{ github.event_name }}"}'

@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+name: history-e2e
+
+on:
+  pull_request:
+    paths:
+      - "src/history/**"
+      - "tests/common/**"
+      - "tests/history_*.rs"
+      - "tests/e2e/run_history_e2e.sh"
+      - ".github/workflows/history-e2e.yml"
+  push:
+    branches: [master, main]
+    paths:
+      - "src/history/**"
+      - "tests/common/**"
+      - "tests/history_*.rs"
+      - "tests/e2e/run_history_e2e.sh"
+      - ".github/workflows/history-e2e.yml"
+  workflow_dispatch:
+
+env:
+  CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
+  RUST_BACKTRACE: 1
+
+jobs:
+  e2e:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
+
+      - name: Build release
+        run: cargo build --release
+
+      - name: Run history E2E
+        run: |
+          chmod +x tests/e2e/run_history_e2e.sh
+          tests/e2e/run_history_e2e.sh 2>&1 | tee history-e2e.log
+
+      - name: Upload logs
+        if: failure()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: history-e2e-logs
+          path: history-e2e.log

@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+# .github/workflows/notify-acfs.yml
+#
+# Notifies ACFS when installer scripts change, triggering checksum updates.
+# Use this template for repos with install.sh at the repository root.
+#
+# Setup:
+#   1. Create a PAT with 'contents:read' on agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
+#   2. Add it as a secret named ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN in this repo
+#   3. Copy this file to .github/workflows/notify-acfs.yml
+#
+# Related: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b04c
+#
+name: Notify ACFS of Installer Changes
+
+on:
+  push:
+    branches: [main, master]
+    paths:
+      - 'install.sh'
+  workflow_dispatch:  # Manual trigger for testing
+
+jobs:
+  notify-acfs:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - name: Trigger ACFS checksum update
+        uses: peter-evans/repository-dispatch@v3
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.ACFS_REPO_DISPATCH_TOKEN }}
+          repository: Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup
+          event-type: upstream-changed
+          client-payload: |
+            {
+              "tool": "${{ github.event.repository.name }}",
+              "ref": "${{ github.ref }}",
+              "sha": "${{ github.sha }}",
+              "actor": "${{ github.actor }}",
+              "timestamp": "${{ github.event.head_commit.timestamp }}"
+            }
+
+      - name: Log dispatch
+        run: |
+          echo "✅ Dispatched upstream-changed event to ACFS"
+          echo "   Tool: ${{ github.event.repository.name }}"
+          echo "   SHA: ${{ github.sha }}"
+          echo "   Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"

 # ============================================================================
 # Heredocs (bash style) - DESTRUCTIVE ONLY
 # ============================================================================
+#
+# NOTE: Heredocs to non-executing commands like `cat`, `tee`, etc. are DATA,
+# not CODE. They are correctly allowed and tested in false_positives/heredoc_data.toml.
+# Only heredocs to executing commands like `bash`, `sh`, `python` should be blocked.
 
 [[case]]
-description = "heredoc containing rm -rf"
-command = "cat <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+description = "heredoc to bash containing git reset"
+command = "bash <<EOF\ngit reset --hard\nEOF"
 expected = "deny"

 [[case]]
-description = "heredoc containing git reset"
-command = "bash <<EOF\ngit reset --hard\nEOF"
+description = "heredoc to sh containing rm -rf"
+command = "sh <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "deny"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Here-strings (<<<) to executing commands - DESTRUCTIVE
+# ============================================================================
+#
+# Here-strings to shells ARE executed and should be blocked.
+
+[[case]]
+description = "bash here-string with rm -rf"
+command = "bash <<<'rm -rf /home/user'"
+expected = "deny"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "sh here-string with git reset"
+command = "sh <<<'git reset --hard'"
 expected = "deny"
 rule_id = "core.git:reset-hard"

@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@
+# False positives: heredocs to non-executing commands
+#
+# These commands use heredocs to pass DATA to non-executing commands like
+# cat, tee, grep, etc. The heredoc content is not executed as code.
+#
+# This is a critical false positive category because:
+# - Documentation often contains dangerous command examples
+# - Log/config files may contain dangerous patterns
+# - These are data, not executable code
+
+# ============================================================================
+# cat heredocs (text output only)
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat heredoc with dangerous-looking rm command is DATA"
+command = "cat <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat heredoc with git reset is DATA"
+command = "cat <<EOF\ngit reset --hard\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat heredoc with multiple dangerous commands is DATA"
+command = "cat <<EOF\nrm -rf /\ngit reset --hard\ndocker system prune -af\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat heredoc with documentation"
+command = "cat <<EOF\n# Dangerous commands to avoid:\n# rm -rf /\n# git reset --hard\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# tee heredocs (file writing)
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "tee heredoc with dangerous content is DATA"
+command = "tee /tmp/docs.txt <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "tee -a heredoc with dangerous content is DATA"
+command = "tee -a /tmp/log.txt <<EOF\ngit reset --hard\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# grep/sed/awk heredocs (text processing)
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "grep heredoc is DATA"
+command = "grep -n 'rm' <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "sed heredoc is DATA"
+command = "sed 's/rm/remove/g' <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "awk heredoc is DATA"
+command = "awk '{print $1}' <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Various text utilities
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "wc heredoc is DATA"
+command = "wc -l <<EOF\nrm -rf /\ngit reset --hard\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "sort heredoc is DATA"
+command = "sort <<EOF\nrm -rf /\ngit clean -fd\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "head heredoc is DATA"
+command = "head -5 <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "tail heredoc is DATA"
+command = "tail -5 <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Encoding utilities
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "base64 heredoc is DATA"
+command = "base64 <<EOF\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "md5sum heredoc is DATA"
+command = "md5sum <<EOF\nrm -rf /\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Network utilities (send data, don't execute)
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "curl -d heredoc is DATA"
+command = "curl -d @- https://example.com <<EOF\nrm -rf /\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Tab-stripped heredocs (<<-)
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat tab-stripped heredoc is DATA"
+command = "cat <<-EOF\n\trm -rf /home/user\n\tEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "tee tab-stripped heredoc is DATA"
+command = "tee /tmp/file <<-MARKER\n\tgit reset --hard\n\tMARKER"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Quoted delimiters (no variable expansion)
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat heredoc with quoted delimiter is DATA"
+command = "cat <<'EOF'\nrm -rf $HOME\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat heredoc with double-quoted delimiter is DATA"
+command = "cat <<\"EOF\"\nrm -rf /home/user\nEOF"
+expected = "allow"
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Here-strings (<<<) to non-executing commands are DATA
+# ============================================================================
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat here-string with single quotes is DATA"
+command = "cat <<<'rm -rf /home/user'"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat here-string with double quotes is DATA"
+command = "cat <<<\"rm -rf /home/user\""
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "cat here-string unquoted is DATA"
+command = "cat <<<dangerous_command"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "grep here-string is DATA"
+command = "grep 'rm' <<<'rm -rf /home/user'"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "tee here-string is DATA"
+command = "tee /tmp/file <<<'git reset --hard'"
+expected = "allow"
+
+[[case]]
+description = "wc here-string is DATA"
+command = "wc -c <<<'rm -rf /'"
+expected = "allow"

+#   ./tests/e2e/run_history_e2e.sh
